data.body:
PASS-1 review — criterion T1 (Prior-art / novel-architecture justification, OVERLAY): CLEAN (no findings).

Affirmations:
- T1(a) prior art considered / not reinventing: The plan builds ISF as one more criterion inside the EXISTING pluggable plan-review criterion framework (criterion = a front-matter prompt file in the da27 prompt library + a routing entry in `criteria_routing.json`), not a new architecture. The registry docstring (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`) already enumerates ISF as a member criterion and documents the `exec: 1-TURN | 2-STEP | AGENT` tiers — so "single-turn/2-STEP, NOT agent" SELECTS an existing tier rather than inventing one. Substantial matching scaffolding already exists: `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_ISF.md` (the ISF prompt), `orchestrator._linked_session_log(...)` with a `summarized` flag, and the "fires only when a session log is linked" gate (`coverage["isf"] = {"ran": False, "reason": "no linked session_log"}`). The oversized-log SUMMARY fallback reuses the existing context-window lever. This is a well-trodden in-house pattern.
- T1(b) novelty justified: no NOVEL architectural pattern is introduced — nothing to justify under Rule-of-Three / anti-repackaging.
- T1(c) unverified capability assertions: none load-bearing; the "frontier model / single-turn / summarize-when-oversized" claims map to existing runner/prompt machinery.

Not-applicable rationale: T1 is an OVERLAY that applies only when a bright line is crossed (external integration, unfamiliar dependency, security/auth, novel architecture, performance/scalability target, migration). None fires here — this is an additive use of an established internal framework. Per T1's anti-FP clause ("a well-trodden pattern needs no prior-art search; not-applicable when no bright-line fires"), this chunk PASSES with no findings.
